nodes.label: Web Typecheck and Build Gate
nodes.rationale: Every branch and pull request installs locked frontend dependencies, typechecks, and builds the web application.

nodes.label: API Lint and Test Gate
nodes.rationale: Every branch and pull request installs runtime and development dependencies, runs Ruff, and executes pytest.

nodes.label: Mandatory Workboard Protocol
nodes.rationale: Agents must coordinate ownership, dependencies, scope, verification, and handoffs through the implementation workboard before editing.

nodes.label: Personal Spending Insight Product Vision
nodes.rationale: The application prioritizes trustworthy, explainable, local financial insight over novelty or cloud functionality.

nodes.label: Profile Isolation
nodes.rationale: Every financial record belongs to exactly one profile, and service-layer filtering prevents data leakage without relying on the frontend.

nodes.label: Archive-First Profile Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: Profile deletion is deferred until deliberate confirmation and export exist; archiving is safer for the first release.

nodes.label: Exact Money and Spending Semantics
nodes.rationale: Integer cents and centralized transaction-type inclusion rules prevent rounding drift and double counting.

nodes.label: Acceptance-Gated Delivery Stages
nodes.rationale: Visual direction, domain correctness, parser reconciliation, and analytics are delivered sequentially behind explicit acceptance gates.

nodes.label: Bounded Agent Delegation
nodes.rationale: Parallel specialists use declared file boundaries and handoff contracts while the lead retains responsibility for accounting, isolation, and reconciliation.

nodes.label: Local-First Application Architecture
nodes.rationale: A loopback FastAPI service, local SQLite store, and browser UI keep private financial data on the user's machine.

nodes.label: Accepted Technology Stack
nodes.rationale: Mainstream typed frontend and backend tools, SQLite, and local app-scoped dependencies balance correctness, maintainability, privacy, and zero-configuration operation.

nodes.label: Meridian Production UI Direction
nodes.rationale: Meridian combines Ledger's information density with Horizon's warmth, rounded cards, gradients, and friendly interactions.

nodes.label: Integer-Cent Money Representation
nodes.rationale: All monetary storage and arithmetic use integer cents so totals remain exact and reproducible.

nodes.label: Transaction Spending Inclusion Policy
nodes.rationale: Purchases drive spending while payments, transfers, income, advances, interest, and fees remain excluded; refunds offset original spending.

nodes.label: Dependency-Ordered Execution
nodes.rationale: Agents claim only ready tasks with completed dependencies, preserve file boundaries, verify acceptance, and append exact handoffs.

nodes.label: Text-Based PDF Parsing Scope
nodes.rationale: TD and Amex selectable-text statements are suitable for issuer-specific deterministic parsing; scanned PDFs are out of scope and CIBC awaits samples.

nodes.label: Statement Fixture Privacy Policy
nodes.rationale: Parser fixtures must be synthetic or minimally redacted, exclude personal financial data, pair inputs with reviewed canonical outputs, and encode money as integer cents.

nodes.label: Excluded Activity Accounting Rule
nodes.rationale: Card payments, transfers, fees, and interest are kept out of spending totals so the financial picture stays honest.

nodes.label: Income and Available-to-Save Summary
nodes.rationale: Estimated income and available-to-save are placed beside spending so the dashboard frames spending in terms of remaining savings capacity.

nodes.label: Excluded Activity Transparency
nodes.rationale: Card payments, transfers, fees, and interest remain visible but are explicitly kept out of spending totals so the financial picture stays auditable and honest.
